description: Guidelines for wiki page tagging — domain tags + aliases for discoverability. Never free-form: every tag must be searchable (user query → keyword match).

tag_principles.domain_tags_first: Primary tags = domain-specific keywords that users would actually type when searching. No generic labels.
tag_principles.avoid_generic_tags: Never use entity, documentation, system, note as standalone tags — they're schema categories (frontmatter.category), not search terms.
tag_principles.searchability_check: Before adding a tag, ask: 'Would this user type this word in their query?' If no → it's architecture metadata, not a tag. Put it in related/sources instead.
tag_principles.tag_count_recommendation: 5-7 domain tags per page + 3-5 aliases for discoverability.

rules: Use concrete product/tech names as tags
Tags describe the idea itself (not 'concept' as a category)
Include related entity tags if applicable
Tags = domain keywords from both compared sources
Cross-link tags should match the entity/concept pages they reference

aliases_system.description: Aliases are discoverability synonyms — words users might type that don't match the page title exactly. They live in frontmatter `aliases` field.
aliases_system.placement_in_frontmatter: aliases should appear after category, before sources/related
aliases_system.example_structure:
---
tags: [llm-wiki, personal-knowledge-base]
date: YYYY-MM-DD
type: documentation
category: entity
aliases: [Loomana, loom, LLM Wiki Pattern, Karpathy wiki]
---
rules: Aliases = product names, synonyms, author names, descriptive phrases users search for
Never put architecture terms (three-layer-architecture) as aliases — they're not searchable
Include both English and native-language variants if page is bilingual

evidence_grade.description: Auto-compute from source authority at ingest time — never manual.

checks_to_add_to_lint_sh.description: Find pages with empty tags [] → suggest domain tags
Flag generic type-tags (entity, documentation) instead of domain-specific ones
